sourceMovie.title: The Pope's Exorcist
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Pope's Exorcist (2023) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Julius Avery's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Pope's Exorcist clicked for you, The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Pope's Exorcist?
faq.answer: Try The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It, Insidious: Chapter 2, The Exorcism of Emily Rose—each shares DNA with The Pope's Exorcist while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Pope's Exorcist?
faq.answer: The Pope's Exorcist (2023) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Pope's Exorcist part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Pope's Exorcist Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
